skill.name: react-nextjs-development
skill.description: Guides React and Next.js 14+ application development with App Router, Server Components, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, testing, and deployment workflows.
skill.summary: Coordinate modern React and Next.js project work from setup through deployment.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command and weak-crypto patterns, but review found they are false positives in Markdown workflow text. No executable scripts, network calls, credential handling, prompt injection attempts, or cryptographic implementation were found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick References
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown references to related skills and copy-paste prompts. They are not shell, Ruby, or JavaScript execution paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown list items or fenced prompt examples. I found no executable command runner, script file, or dynamic command construction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections are prose or frontend terminology. No hashing, encryption, or cipher API usage appears in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are documentation about React, styling, design, and responsive checks. There is no cryptographic implementation to evaluate.

content.value_statement: Modern React projects need consistent setup, architecture, testing, and deployment decisions. This skill organizes Next.js and React work into clear phases for AI-assisted development.
actual_capabilities: Guides project setup for React and Next.js 14+ applications.
Organizes component architecture, state management, forms, routing, and styling tasks.
Recommends related specialized skills for each development phase.
Defines quality gates for TypeScript, linting, tests, performance, accessibility, and responsive design.
Covers testing and Vercel deployment workflow steps.
limitations: It is a workflow guide and does not include executable project templates.
It references related skills that may not be installed in every environment.
It does not replace framework documentation for version-specific changes.
It does not audit generated application code for production security.

use_cases.title: Start a New Product Interface
use_cases.description: Plan a new Next.js application with App Router, TypeScript, styling, forms, tests, and deployment steps.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Delivery
use_cases.description: Give a team a shared workflow for building components, routes, data fetching, and quality checks.

use_cases.title: Guide AI Pair Programming
use_cases.description: Use focused prompts to ask Claude, Codex, or Claude Code for work on each frontend phase.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a New App
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a Next.js 14 application with App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, forms, tests, and Vercel deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Design Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my product requirements and propose a React component structure, state boundaries, reusable UI components, and custom hooks.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Data Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Next.js feature and recommend Server Components, client components, API routes, caching, loading states, and error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Release
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a release readiness plan for my React app covering TypeScript, linting, tests, accessibility, performance, responsive behavior, and deployment.

output_examples.input: I need to build a dashboard in Next.js.
output_examples.output: A phased plan covering project setup, layout structure, reusable components, data fetching, form handling, tests, and Vercel deployment checks.

output_examples.input: My React app needs better architecture.
output_examples.output: A component and state management review with recommendations for composition, hooks, shared UI elements, and clear client-server boundaries.

output_examples.input: We are ready to ship a frontend release.
output_examples.output: A quality gate checklist for compilation, linting, tests, web vitals, accessibility, responsive behavior, and preview deployment review.

best_practices: Use the workflow phase that matches the current task instead of applying every phase at once.
Verify referenced skills and tools are available before relying on their prompts.
Run framework tests, accessibility checks, and performance checks before deployment.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the workflow as a complete security review for application code.
Do not skip TypeScript, linting, and tests because the project builds locally.
Do not mix Server Components and client state without clear ownership boundaries.

faq.question: Does this skill create a complete Next.js project?
faq.answer: No. It provides a workflow and prompts for guiding project creation and implementation.

faq.question: Which Next.js features does it emphasize?
faq.answer: It emphasizes App Router, Server Components, data fetching, routing, forms, testing, and Vercel deployment.

faq.question: Can I use it with a React SPA?
faq.answer: Yes. The setup phase includes React SPA work, though many later steps focus on Next.js.

faq.question: Does it support Tailwind CSS?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Tailwind CSS setup, design tokens, utility classes, component styles, and responsive design checks.

faq.question: Is this a security auditing skill?
faq.answer: No. It includes quality gates, but it does not perform security testing of generated application code.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code prompts.
